metadata.title: EFFECTIVENESS AND IMPORTANCE OF MODERN PEDAGOGICAL TECHNOLOGIES IN TEACHING CHEMISTRY IN SCHOOL EDUCATION
metadata.description: This article provides a deep scientific analysis of the role and effectiveness of modern pedagogical technologies in teaching chemistry in secondary schools. It is argued that the fundamental reforms taking place in the educational process in the 21st century, digitalization processes, the use of interactive methods and digital tools aimed at developing active thinking of students have a positive impact on the effective mastery of chemistry. The article covers the use of modern technologies in chemistry lessons, their didactic and psychological aspects, their importance in forming independent thinking of students, as well as the advantages of modern methods such as problem-based learning, project approach, STEAM integration. Based on the analysis of existing scientific literature, the author draws conclusions on the impact of modern technologies on real educational outcomes and puts forward promising proposals.

creators.name: Tojiboyeva Munisa Akmal qizi
creators.affiliation: Chirchik State Pedagogical University Faculty of Natural Sciences 3rd year student of Chemistry Education

metadata.title: Analysis of Diagnostic Accuracy Measures
metadata.description: This Demonstration shows plots of various diagnostic accuracy measures for diagnostic tests in normally distributed nondiseased and diseased populations. This is done for different values of disease prevalence, taking into account the means and standard deviations of the populations. The means and standard deviations are expressed in arbitrary units. The measures considered are the positive predictive value (PPV), the negative predictive value (NPV), the (diagnostic) odds ratio (OR), the likelihood ratio for a positive test result (LR+), and the likelihood ratio for a negative test result (LR-). The measures are plotted against the sensitivity (Se) or the specificity (Sp) of the test, which can be selected by clicking the respective button.
metadata.notes: The Demonstration can be run on Wolfram Player® or Wolfram Mathematica®

metadata.title: The Role of Digitalization in Improving the Performance of Microcredit Institutions in Morocco
metadata.description:
This research analyzes the impact of digitalization on the performance of microcredit institutions in Morocco. By focusing on the automation of operations, digital access, cybersecurity, and the expansion of mobile services and online platforms, the study evaluates how these dimensions influence efficiency, cost reduction, and financial inclusion in the microfinance sector.
Data was collected through interviews with executives and questionnaires sent to various key stakeholders within microcredit institutions. The data analysis, conducted using multivariate statistical tools, revealed that the digitalization of operations significantly improves operational efficiency by automating processes and reducing processing times. Digital access has expanded financial inclusion by facilitating access to services for marginalized populations, while cybersecurity enhances customer trust by protecting sensitive data.
The results show that the expansion of mobile services and online platforms reduces operational costs by decreasing reliance on physical branches and optimizing management processes. In conclusion, digitalization constitutes a crucial lever for improving the performance of microcredit institutions, with significant implications for efficiency, accessibility, and security. Institutions should continue to invest in these technologies to enhance their impact and support greater financial inclusion.

Keywords: Digitalization, Automation, Digital Access, Cybersecurity, Performance Improvement, Microcredit Institutions in Morocco.

journal.title: International Journal of Economic Studies and Management (IJESM) -ISSN 2789-049X

data.title: The Role of Digitalization in Improving the Performance of Microcredit Institutions in Morocco

metadata.title: THE IMPACT OF AI-POWERED TOOLS ON STUDENTS' READING DEVELOPMENT
metadata.description: Artificial Intelligence has become one of the most influential technologies in modern education, especially in language learning and literacy development. AI-powered educational tools provide personalized, adaptive, and interactive learning environments that significantly improve students’ reading comprehension skills. This article examines the role of AI in enhancing students’ reading abilities, focusing on AI-based learning platforms, speech-recognition systems, conversational chatbots, and adaptive reading technologies. The study also discusses the educational benefits of AI, including personalized learning, vocabulary development, learner motivation, critical thinking enhancement, and inclusive education. Furthermore, the article analyzes challenges related to AI integration in educational settings, such as technological limitations, ethical concerns, and the need for teacher training. The findings demonstrate that AI technologies contribute positively to students’ reading performance and create effective learning environments that support academic success in the digital era.

creators.name: Ko'charova Rukhshona Shavkatovna
creators.affiliation: The student of Uzbekistan State World Languages University

keywords: Artificial Intelligence, reading comprehension, ESL learners, adaptive learning, educational technology, literacy development.

metadata.title: Fragebögen bezüglich des Einflusses von Prädispositionen auf räumliche Präsenz
metadata.description:
Übersicht

Die XML-Dateien beinhalten Fragebögen aus dem Tool "umfragenup.uni-potsdam.de", die im Rahmen der Doktorarbeit von Axel Wiepke entwickelt wurden. Die Identifizierungsfragen wurden genutzt, um ein Pseudonym für Proband*innen zu erstellen und mehrere Fragebögenergebnisse miteinander zu verknüpfen. Der PreQuestionnaire beinhaltet Fragen zu Prädispositionen, die räumliche Präsenz laut einer Literaturrecherche beeinflussen. Der PosttestUXIVE beinhaltet Fragen zu erfahrener räumlicher Präsenz und weiteren Qualitäten der Userexperience. Am Ende der Studie wurden Proband*innen noch die FreitextFeedback fragen gestellt, um Freitext-Rückmeldungen sammeln zu können.

Die R Datei wurde entwickelt, um die Antworten auf die Fragen im .csv-Format deskriptiv, korrelativ und visuell auszuwerten. Eine Publikation der Tools ist zum jetzigen Zeitpunkt noch nicht geschehen. Erste Ergebnisse reproduzieren jedoch die Validierung der Einzelfragebögen und die erwarteten Zusammenhänge der Prädispositionen und Präsenz.

PreQuestionnaire

Der folgende Fragebogen wurde erstellt, um die, in der Literaturrecherche identifizierten Kategorien in einem Messwerkzeug zu bündeln.
Dabei wurden den Variablen Items aus standardisierten Fragebögen wie folgt entnommen:

  • Domain Specific Interest und Visual Spatial Imagery beschreiben das Interesse am Inhalt der dargestellten Inhalte und die kognitiven Fähigkeiten der mentalen Organisation räumlicher Signale. Beide Variablen wurden als Prädiktoren für räumliche Präsenz auch bereits im Rahmen des MEC-Modells vorhergesehen. So wurde ein Fragebogen entwickelt, der diese Variablen beinhaltet. Vorderer, P, Wirth, W., Gouveia, F. R., Biocca, F., Saari, T., Jäncke, F., Böcking, S.,Schramm, H., Gysbers, A., Hartmann, T., Klimmt, C., Laarni, J., Ravaja, N., Sacau, A.,Baumgartner, T. & Jäncke, P. (2004). MEC Spatial Presence Questionnaire (MECSPQ):Short Documentation and Instructions for Application. Report to the EuropeanCommunity, Project Presence: MEC (IST-2001-37661). Online. Available fromhttp://www.ijk.hmt-hannover.de/presence.
  • Agreeableness beschreibt das Ausmaß, in dem Personen mitfühlend, vertrauensvoll, kooperativ und rücksichtsvoll gegenüber anderen sind, anstatt misstrauisch, wettbewerbsorientiert oder antagonistisch zu handeln. Costa, P. T. Jr., & McCrae, R. R. (1992). Revised NEO Personality Inventory (NEO-PI-R) and NEO Five-Factor Inventory (NEO-FFI) professional manual. Odessa, FL: Psychological Assessment Resources.
  • Absorption bezeichnet die Neigung, sich intensiv und aufmerksam in Wahrnehmungen, Vorstellungen oder Erfahrungen zu vertiefen, sodass diese als besonders lebendig, bedeutsam oder real erlebt werden. Bestehende Fragebögen für das Persönlichkeitsmerkmal Absorption sind sehr umfangreich. Laidlaw, T. M., Dwivedi, P., Naito, A., & Gruzelier, J. H. (2005). Low self-directedness (TCI), mood, schizotypy and hypnotic susceptibility. Personality and Individual Differences, 39(2), 469–480.
  • Self-Transcendence bezeichnet die Neigung, die Grenzen des eigenen Selbst zu überschreiten und Erfahrungen von Verbundenheit mit umfassenderen sozialen, natürlichen oder spirituellen Zusammenhängen zu erleben. Self-Transcendence korreliert stark mit Absorption und bietet einen validierten Fragebogen. Haugen, G., Rannestad, T., Garåsen, H., Hammervold, R., Espnes, G., A. (2011) The Self-Transcendence Scale: An Investigation of the Factor Strucuture Among Nursing Home Patients. jhn, (S. 1-6). https://doi.org/10.1177/0898010111429849

Daraus ergibt sich der bisher unvalidierte Fragebogen mit jeweils 5 Punkte Likert-Skala Items.

PostTextUXIVE

Zur Messung von räumlicher Präsenz und weiteren Faktoren der UX, wurde der Fragebogen \emph{User Experience in Immersive Virtual Environments} (UXIVE) konzipiert. Tcha-Tokey, K., Loup-Escande, E., Christmann, O., Richir, S. (2016) A Questionnaire to Measure the User Experience in Immersive Virtual Environments. VRIC, 16 (S. 1-5). Laval, Frankreich. https://doi.org/10.1145/2927929.2927955
In der Validierung des Fragebogens wurden 15 Fragen nicht durch die Pearson-Korrelation gestützt. Tcha-Tokey, K., Christmann, O., Loup-Escande, E., Loup, G., Richir, S. (2018) Towards a Model of User Experience in Immersive Virtual Environments. Hindawi, 2018. Frankreich. https://doi.org/10.1155/2018/7827286
Diese 15 Fragen wurden aus dem Fragebogen entfernt.
Die verbleibenden Fragen wurden vom Autor übersetzt und die Übersetzung von zwei Personen auf Fehler geprüft. Durch teilweise unübliche Formulierungen im Englischen erscheinen einige Formulierungen im Deutschen auch unüblich.

metadata.title: SCIENCE BFSP CoS | WP3 | D3.3 Tutorial hosted on the Galaxy Training Network and visible on ELIXIR' TeSS
metadata.description:
This deliverable covers the development of training materials supporting the Metagenome-Assembled Genomes (MAGs) generation workflows created by the FAIRyMAGs project. 

To achieve this, a complete learning pathway and a comprehensive tutorial were developed and published in the Galaxy Training Network (GTN), guiding trainees step-by-step through all tasks required to generate MAGs. The learning pathway links in a comprehensive way different hands-on tutorials that cover in depth essential steps, from quality control and read preprocessing to assembly, binning, refinement, and annotation of MAGs. The tutorials used in the learning pathway correspond to both updated and expanded versions of existing GTN training materials, as well as newly developed content.

metadata.title: pyGCodeDecode
metadata.description: For the analysis of GCode, slicers or dedicated analyzer tools, such as Prusa Slicer or  gCodeViewer, merely display target process parameters. The actual process velocity however is  determined by the firmware of the printer. Velocities are expected to deviate significantly from  the target velocity at times of acceleration and deceleration. pyGCodeDecode aims to model the  machine's actual behavior and visualize its influence.

creators.name: Knirsch, Jonathan
creators.affiliation: Institute of Vehicle System Technology - Lightweight Engineering, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT), Rintheimer Querallee 2, Karlsruhe, 76131, Germany

metadata.notes: This package reads the target trajectory firmware settings changes from a GCode file. Subsequently, it simulates a motion planner with acceleration and jerk or junction control respectively. The more accurate modeling is achieved by replicating grbl's and its derivatives' firmware-specific movement planner solutions, such as "Classic Jerk" and "Junction Deviation", as an interpretation for Jerk. The simulation result is a description of the nozzle and extrusion axis position and velocity over time and is easily accessible for further analysis. For example it can be used to generate time dependent boundary conditions, needed in additive manufacturing simulations for the Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) process. The package includes 3D plotting functions, so it can be used to visualize local velocities before printing to improve your process understanding.
